scenarios.opening_query: A delivery driver on my route has started lingering at my door and commenting on my schedule.
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's not a random driver — he's my ex-boyfriend who took that route on purpose after we broke up; we lived together for a year.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's my ex — he took that route deliberately after our breakup. We lived together a year.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's a total stranger — the company confirmed he even swapped shifts to get my street.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: A stranger — the company says he swapped shifts to get my street.
scenarios.vetting_note: Route-manipulation fact common to both; relationship routes.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband's ex violated the protective order again and the police just 'took a report.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || General Litigation > Stalking Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: The order protects my husband — but she's now targeting ME with the following and the notes, and I have no relationship with her at all; my own petition is the stalking-order kind.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The existing order protects my husband — she's now targeting ME, and I have no relationship with her. My petition is the stalking-order kind.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: who needs the new protection and their relationship to the actor
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's still my husband she's harassing — enforcement and renewal of HIS order is the need.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's still my husband she targets — enforcement and renewal of his order.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter's volleyball coach texts her constantly and the messages have crossed every line.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || Family Law > Juvenile/DHS Issues || General Litigation > Child Abuse Victims
scenarios.hidden_fact: She's 14 — we reported to police and the school; a mandatory child-abuse investigation is open, and the protective steps run through that system.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's 14 — police and the school are involved, and a mandatory child-abuse investigation is open. Protection runs through that system.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the target is a minor with an open investigation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She's 19 and on the college team — an adult seeking her own stalking order against him.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She's 19, on the college team — she'll seek her own order.

scenarios.opening_query: The man I reported for vandalizing my car has now taken to standing across from my shop for hours.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || General Litigation > Crime Victims
scenarios.hidden_fact: He was convicted of felony mischief for the car — the standing-around began after sentencing, and my main goal now is the civil damages case the conviction makes easy, alongside safety.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He was convicted for the car — and now I mainly want the civil damages case the conviction supports, alongside safety.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a conviction supports a victim damages claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Charges never stuck — and the watching is escalating; the stalking order is my priority, damages later.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Charges never stuck — and the watching is escalating. Order first, damages later.

scenarios.opening_query: My brother's ex keeps 'accidentally' running into him and now shows up wherever our whole family goes.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Restraining Orders || General Litigation > Stalking Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: My brother won't seek anything — he minimizes it all. The petitions worth filing are MINE and my mother's, and neither of us ever had any relationship with her, so ours are stalking orders.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My brother won't act — the petitions worth filing are mine and my mother's, and we never had any relationship with her. Stalking orders for us.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which family members will petition and their relationships to the actor
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My brother's finally ready to act himself — they lived together three years, so his petition is the family-abuse order.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's finally ready to act himself — three years living together makes his the family-abuse order.
scenarios.vetting_note: Petitioner-identity fact: the reluctant qualified victim vs willing unqualified relatives.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone keeps unlocking my smart locks remotely and I wake up to my front door ajar.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Stalking Orders || Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law || Family Law > Restraining Orders
scenarios.hidden_fact: The lock company's logs show access from my ex-husband's account credentials that were never removed — the unauthorized-access case and forcing the company to secure my account are the immediate legal work.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The logs show my ex-husband's leftover credentials doing it — the unauthorized-access case and locking down the account are the immediate work.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether documented digital intrusion is the mechanism
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The logs show physical bump-key entry, no digital access — and a neighbor's camera caught a stranger I've now identified.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's physical entry — a neighbor's camera caught a stranger I've identified.
